text:
**The trap to avoid:** the docs themselves warn (tenet #8, value-layers) that "stop agents clobbering each other" is the *seductive demo*, not the foundation. The defensible anchor is **identity + awareness** — "promote your agents from processes to citizens." [...] - I did NOT find "agents stop clobbering each other and start covering for each other" verbatim anywhere in current docs — closest is value-layers.md:14 "two agents stop clobbering each other and start covering for each other" — actually IS present verbatim, attributed as "the product instinct" (one of two competing framings, explicitly flagged as the LESS load-bearing, more "over-claimed" one per the doc's own skeptic rebuttal). Use with caution — the doc itself argues against leading with this. [...] What IS grounded/shipped: the actual acute pain (problem-space.md) is pains #1–3 — human-as-glue tax, amnesiac/non-portable agents, app silos — explicitly marked **acute** (felt daily, today) vs. pains #6–8 (push-not-pull, cross-person coordination, provenance) marked **aspirational**. Pain #5 ("multiple agents, zero shared awareness" — the dev-fleet collision story) is explicitly flagged: "the pain the dev-fleet framing over-indexes on. It's real but narrow" and "the seductive demo we must not let define the project" (problem-space.md:82,85).

So: the honest target user today is a **solo developer running multiple coding-agent hosts (Claude Code + Codex + …) who wants durable identity and shared awareness across sessions/devices** — but the docs themselves warn against over-indexing on the "agents collide on the same file" framing as the pitch, since that's unproven (see Bets Q1) and explicitly demoted to "ceiling, not floor" in value-layers.md. [...] 8. Coordination is an experiment, not a pillar (explicitly hedges the flashiest feature)
